item.title: Community Tech And Governance
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Community Tech & Governance.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Engadget] Apple and Google are reportedly pointing users to 'nudify' apps and [Business Insider] The newest prediction markets guru is a middle schooler in braces, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How does opacity in institutional decision-making undermine democratic accountability in rural communities, and what information infrastructure can address it? Topics include: Federal access to information proposals and exemptions for 'transitory' records, TRU Williams Lake campus closure as a governance accountability gap, Irish fuel protests as a case study in civic breakdown from opaque decision-making, Proactive disclosure and shared transparency infrastructure models for rural municipalities, Regional coordination mechanisms through the Cariboo Regional District.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Community Futures Cariboo Chilcotin

item.title: Working Lands And Industry
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Working Lands & Industry.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Engadget] Deezer says AI-made songs make up 44 percent of daily uploads and [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. to opt into part of federal temporary foreign worker expansion, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Can electric equipment effectively replace gas-powered tools in Cariboo working landscapes, and who bears the costs and risks of that transition? Topics include: Electric off-road motorcycles (KTM Freeride E) for ranch operations, Total cost of ownership and maintenance differentials between electric and gas equipment, Rural service infrastructure and field-serviceability of electric vehicles, Workforce development and skills transition for electric drivetrain repair, Collective procurement and co-operative models for equipment transition.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

item.title: Arts, Culture And Digital Storytelling
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. premier’s office says DRIPA changes will not be introduced this spring after all and [100 Mile Free Press] Cancer patient donates saddle sale to 100 Mile House oncology department, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can rural Cariboo communities build lasting digital storytelling capacity that preserves cultural memory while maintaining sovereignty over their own narratives? Topics include: Digitization infrastructure vs. storytelling capacity building, Cultural sovereignty and governance in digital archiving, Interpretive labor and the role of elders in meaning-making, Technology accessibility for rural communities, Institutional partnerships and power dynamics in cultural preservation.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

item.title: Science, Wonder And The Natural World
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Science, Wonder & the Natural World.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [ScienceAlert] Tiny Microbes Hiding in Soil May Help Pull Rain From The Sky, Study Reveals and [New Atlas] This all-female fish species reproduces without sex by cloning itself, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can rural communities develop the interpretive capacity to convert scientific findings into local ecological and economic decisions? Topics include: The gap between scientific knowledge production and local implementation in rural regions, Integration of Indigenous/Traditional Ecological Knowledge with Western science while preserving integrity, Role of extension services and knowledge broker models in translating research, Cariboo rural resilience mechanisms and economic diversity evolution, Community interpretive capacity as prerequisite to ecological governance and decision-making authority.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Community Futures Cariboo Chilcotin

item.title: Cariboo Voices And Local News
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Cariboo Voices & Local News.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [100 Mile Free Press] 100 Mile fire crews douse blaze that spread between yards on Kennedy Road and [100 Mile Free Press] FORSTER: Another storage shed claimed by fire at Mountain Spruce Community Centre, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How do small rural communities like those in the Cariboo actually make public decisions, and are the formal mechanisms available to them functioning effectively? Topics include: Williams Lake cannabis licensing decision as a case study in local democratic procedure versus substance, Canada Post service changes as an example of decisions made remotely without meaningful local input, Civic technology tools (meeting transcription, decision dashboards, participatory budgeting) and their applicability to small municipalities, The gap between consultation and genuine decision-making power in rural governance, Indigenous governance models (Tŝilhqot'in Nation) as contrasts to current non-Indigenous local democratic processes.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

item.title: Wild Spaces And Outdoor Life
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [100 Mile Free Press] Springs events ramping up in Lac La Hache and [Williams Lake Tribune] ‘Surreal’: Logan Lake resident wins $1M in lottery, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can technology actually protect communities during acute emergencies (floods, fires) when the gap between notification systems and genuine preparedness is fundamentally social and structural, not technical? Topics include: Gaps between notification systems (AlertBC) and genuine community preparedness in the acute emergency moment, How community-built emergency management systems outperform vendor-imposed platforms in rural and Indigenous contexts, Nature-based flood solutions (wetland restoration, floodplain reconnection) enabled by LiDAR and remote sensing technology, and their political economy implications, Indigenous territorial governance as enabling long-term ecological planning and data stewardship across generations, Data sovereignty and institutional continuity in environmental monitoring and emergency management.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

item.title: Indigenous Lands And Innovation
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Indigenous Lands & Innovation.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down Preventing AI extractivism: the case for braiding indigenous data justice with ABS for stronger AI data governance and TMU students to safeguard Residential School Survivor testimonies, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: What infrastructure, governance, and safeguards must be in place before Indigenous communities build digital archives to ensure long-term functionality, community control, and protection from data extraction? Topics include: Format obsolescence and technical sovereignty in digital preservation, Shared infrastructure governance models versus isolated community systems, AI extractivism and machine-readable metadata protection (TK Labels), Temporal asymmetry between knowledge preservation and data extraction, Regional Indigenous consortium potential for shared technical capacity in the Cariboo.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Denisiqi Services Society

item.title: Community Tech And Governance
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Community Tech & Governance.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down Immersed in Community (and Archives!) and Immersed in Community (and Archives!), plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: What does it mean for communities—particularly Indigenous nations in BC—to govern technology itself, rather than simply using it, and what institutional capacity is required to sustain that governance? Topics include: Distinction between using technology for governance versus governing technology itself, Design sustainability: the tension between data sovereignty restrictions and emergency technical support access, Archival governance and the asymmetric stakes of data loss for Indigenous nations versus municipalities, Funding models: grant dependency versus revenue-generating data governance and education-based capacity pipelines, The relationship between overall institutional health and digital governance capacity.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

item.title: Working Lands And Industry
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Working Lands & Industry.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [The Tyee] Cattle Were Poisoned by BC’s Forest Fertilizer. Now Someone Will Pay and [BC Gov News] Aerial spraying will curb invasive spongy moths in Delta, Squamish, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Can mining governance and technology models be redesigned to ensure companies bear long-term liability for environmental damage rather than leaving taxpayers and communities holding the reclamation bill? Topics include: Bond calculation and reclamation cost estimation failures in BC mining history, Environmental monitoring technology and real-time data access as accountability tools, Indigenous co-governance arrangements and decision authority in mining oversight, Post-closure liability and orphan site funding models, Community technical capacity gaps during environmental assessment processes.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Cariboo Agricultural Research Alliance

item.title: Arts, Culture And Digital Storytelling
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down #1334 The Long Fight for a Little Justice in Burns Lake and #172 Why Governments Want to Ban Kids From AI and Social Media, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: What foundational conditions must exist in rural communities before digital storytelling infrastructure can meaningfully address the underrepresentation of their narratives? Topics include: The gap between cultural production capacity and platform/distribution infrastructure, Distinguishing between grant-dependent tools versus sustainable internal capacity-building, Indigenous communities' approach to language documentation as self-sustaining storytelling practice, Existing Cariboo cultural institutions as underutilized infrastructure nodes, Long-term custodianship and archival accessibility as core design principle.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Cariboo Chilcotin Partners for Literacy

item.title: Science, Wonder And The Natural World
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Science, Wonder & the Natural World.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] Volunteers, students plant 500 trees to rejuvenate B.C. park after 2023 wildfire and [XDA Developers] The Linux kernel now allows AI-written code, but you're on the hook for it, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can rural communities transition from being passive data sources for external scientific projects to becoming active generators and owners of ecological research questions and findings? Topics include: Data sovereignty and the OCAP principles in community science partnerships, Knowledge extraction versus epistemic collaboration with Indigenous communities, Sustainable funding and governance models for long-term ecological monitoring, Role of technology (drones, sensors) in making community-scale ecological research economically viable, Community Futures organizations as potential intermediaries for ecological enterprise and stewardship compensation.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Community Futures Cariboo Chilcotin

item.title: Cariboo Voices And Local News
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Cariboo Voices & Local News.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] B.C. unemployment rate spikes 0.6 percentage points to 6.7 per cent in March and [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. government easing carbon tax for pulp mills as industry grapples with future, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: What constitutes community resilience infrastructure in rural Cariboo communities, and who actually maintains it? Topics include: Fire response infrastructure and volunteer emergency services, Air quality monitoring and vulnerable population access to health information, Cultural institutions as psychological resilience resources, Community foundation endowment models for sustaining local infrastructure, Indigenous Guardian programs as models for sustained locally-rooted capacity.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

item.title: Wild Spaces And Outdoor Life
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] New Cariboo fire agreement aims to cut emergency response delays and [CNET] US Mobile Is Getting Into the Home Internet Business by Pairing With Starlink, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Can technology effectively support Indigenous-led wildfire management and cultural burning practices, or does it risk substituting for community knowledge and authority? Topics include: Role of Indigenous women in reclaiming cultural burning practices and dual expertise as firefighters and cultural values experts, Funding architecture and governance models for Nation-led land stewardship versus project-based grant cycles, Integration of fire behavior modeling technology with traditional ecological knowledge in prescribed burn planning, Data sovereignty, OCAP principles, and knowledge extraction risks in academic-Indigenous community partnerships, Restoration of pedagogical and social structures for knowledge transmission alongside technical practice restoration.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Cariboo Chilcotin Conservation Society

Topics include: Role of Indigenous women in reclaiming cultural burning practices and dual expertise as firefighters and cultural values experts, Funding architecture and governance models for Nation-led land stewardship versus project-based grant cycles, Integration of fire behavior modeling technology with traditional ecological knowledge in prescribed burn planning, Data sovereignty, OCAP principles, and knowledge extraction risks in academic-Indigenous community partnerships, Restoration of pedagogical and social structures for knowledge transmission alongside technical practice restoration.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

item.title: Indigenous Lands And Innovation
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Indigenous Lands & Innovation.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. First Nation at odds over ownership of historic Greater Victoria site and [Williams Lake Tribune] Barkerville 2026 season brings gold rush history to life with new attractions and events, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: When Indigenous communities share ecological knowledge with Western institutions, how can that sharing preserve Indigenous data sovereignty and prevent knowledge systems from being absorbed or devalued? Topics include: Data sovereignty and decolonizing data systems, Knowledge braiding versus integration, Indigenous fire stewardship and clam garden restoration, Traditional ecological knowledge versus Western scientific monitoring, Food sovereignty and ecosystem restoration projects, Funding gaps in Guardian Stewards and community-controlled programs.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

item.title: Community Tech And Governance
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Community Tech & Governance.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] Annual cleaning of water mains and reservoirs begins in Williams Lake and [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] UPDATE: 3 arts positions reinstated at Okanagan College following arbitration, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: What governance failures are revealed when Indigenous communities must resort to litigation to protect their territories from industrial contamination, and what infrastructure would enable proactive rather than reactive protection? Topics include: Monitoring authority and data control as governance levers, Litigation as a governance mechanism when regulatory systems fail, Funding sustainability for nation-led environmental infrastructure, The gap between legal rights frameworks and operational capacity to exercise them, Treaty negotiation versus alternative pathways to jurisdiction.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

item.title: Working Lands And Industry
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Working Lands & Industry.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] Victoria seeks federal, provincial input to develop ‘reasonable guardrails’ for AI and [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake businesses and homeowners encouraged to FireSmart, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can BC's regulatory framework be restructured to prevent harm from overlapping Crown land uses (forestry, ranching, mining) rather than simply compensating victims after the fact? Topics include: Cattle poisoning settlement as precedent and its limitations, Data sovereignty and documentation as leverage in resource disputes, Crown land fragmentation across provincial ministries and coordination failures, Indigenous co-governance and First Nations' potential conflict resolution role, Organized collective bargaining versus atomized individual permit holders.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

item.title: Arts, Culture And Digital Storytelling
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] Adam Sandler spotted in B.C. while filming movie and [Williams Lake Tribune] Robert Barron column: I hope I get to see humans walk on Mars, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How should rural and Indigenous communities build digital cultural infrastructure that serves their storytelling needs while avoiding both corporate platform gatekeeping and unsustainable technological dependence? Topics include: Ireland's government digital wallet age verification system and identity gatekeeping implications, Algorithmic attention economics as a form of cultural gatekeeping versus government-mandated identity systems, Data sovereignty and Indigenous control over cultural archival materials, Regional digital cultural infrastructure co-op model with shared hosting and technical capacity, Digital preservation sustainability and technology obsolescence risks for cultural projects.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Central Cariboo Arts & Culture Society

item.title: Resilient Rural Futures
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Resilient Rural Futures.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Hackaday] The Smallest Dialup ISP is a Raspberry Pi and a Prison Phone and [XDA Developers] A single mini PC replaced my Raspberry Pi cluster and nearly cut my power use in half, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: When precision agriculture technology works effectively, how should rural farmers engage with the data ownership and platform dependency structures that come with it? Topics include: Data ownership and control in precision agriculture platforms, The asymmetry between farmer data generation and platform value extraction, Cooperative versus corporate models for agricultural data governance, Technology dependency risks and platform obsolescence in rural contexts, Practical negotiation strategies for rural technology adoption.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Quesnel Community Foundation

item.itunes:summary:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Resilient Rural Futures.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Hackaday] The Smallest Dialup ISP is a Raspberry Pi and a Prison Phone and [XDA Developers] A single mini PC replaced my Raspberry Pi cluster and nearly cut my power use in half, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: When precision agriculture technology works effectively, how should rural farmers engage with the data ownership and platform dependency structures that come with it? Topics include: Data ownership and control in precision agriculture platforms, The asymmetry between farmer data generation and platform value extraction, Cooperative versus corporate models for agricultural data governance, Technology dependency risks and platform obsolescence in rural contexts, Practical negotiation strategies for rural technology adoption.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

item.title: Cariboo Voices And Local News
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Cariboo Voices & Local News.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] Prince George RCMP investigate after object strikes paramedic inside ambulance and [Hackaday] Reverse-Engineering a Handheld Car Tire Pressure Gauge, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can local accountability infrastructure—particularly journalism—be sustainably funded and structured to serve rural communities like the Cariboo? Topics include: Elections BC enforcement and formal political accountability mechanisms, Local journalism sustainability and the business model crisis in rural media, First Nations governance and Indigenous data sovereignty as parallel accountability structures, Civic tech tools and their sustainability challenges in rural contexts, Community foundation endowment models as durable funding alternatives.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

item.title: Wild Spaces And Outdoor Life
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [The Tyee] A Deadly Fungal Invader Is Threatening BC’s Bats and [BC Gov News] Opening Clean Industry Fund 2026 intake, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Who controls ecological knowledge and data in Cariboo wilderness, and does technology democratization actually distribute power or concentrate it further? Topics include: Data sovereignty and Indigenous data governance frameworks (OCAP principles, UNDRIP implementation), Co-management models for monitoring infrastructure versus province-run systems, Citizen science limitations and strengths in low-population-density rural regions, Backcountry safety infrastructure (satellite communicators, SAR funding models), Maintenance and sustainability of remote monitoring equipment in harsh climates.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

item.title: Indigenous Lands And Innovation
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Indigenous Lands & Innovation.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Engadget] What’s going on with Donut Lab? and [Williams Lake Tribune] British Columbians declare 15,600 assault-style firearms in buyback program, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: What does it mean for technology and institutional processes to genuinely serve Indigenous self-determination rather than adding complexity or enabling external control? Topics include: DRIPA implementation failures and capacity constraints in consultation processes, Indigenous language revitalization technology and maintenance sustainability, Data sovereignty and environmental monitoring with regulatory authority, Community-owned versus externally-deployed infrastructure models, Maintenance funding as the critical but underfunded layer of technology sustainability.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

item.title: Community Tech And Governance
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Community Tech & Governance.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [BC Gov News] Building volunteer fire department capacity and [Android Authority] Android Auto just made EV trip planning a lot smarter for these car brands, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How do information systems and governance structures in rural communities determine who has access to decision-making power, and whose interests do transparency measures actually serve? Topics include: Cougar alert systems and emergency communication infrastructure in rural BC, Canada Post mail delivery consolidation and loss of bundled community services, Aboriginal Title Transparency Act and strategic disclosure in Indigenous governance, Epistemic infrastructure and who controls information about community needs, Heiltsuk and other Indigenous-led information sovereignty models.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

item.title: Working Lands And Industry
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Working Lands & Industry.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [The Tyee] Half of BC’s Community Paramedic Positions Are Unfilled and [Williams Lake Tribune] New Democrats pass resolution to protect northwest B.C. coastal tanker ban, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can the Cariboo build a working landscape that is economically viable, ecologically sustainable, and socially equitable when these three goals are often in tension? Topics include: BC Timber Sales policy design and competitive market dynamics in forestry, Soil health science, microbial inoculants, and measurement challenges for ranching, Carbon markets, ecosystem service payments, and aggregation models for smallholder operations, Indigenous co-governance, traditional ecological knowledge, and tenure restructuring, Infrastructure gaps: connectivity, processing facilities, long-term capital, and organizational capacity.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

item.title: Wild Spaces And Outdoor Life
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Boing Boing] Amazing camera trap footage represents hope for endangered tigers and [Android Authority] Amazfit just launched its biggest watch yet, and it’s packed for navigating, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Is advancing technology genuinely improving backcountry safety accessibility for rural communities, or is it creating new barriers and false confidence? Topics include: Technology equity and affordability barriers, Rural connectivity and infrastructure challenges, Training program sustainability and access, Technology literacy versus traditional backcountry skills, Community-based implementation strategies.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

item.title: Indigenous Lands And Innovation
item.description: Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Indigenous Lands & Innovation. NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Nautilus] Space Age Technology Reveals Secrets of Bronze Age Sword and [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] B.C. quietly suspends major affordable housing fund, leaving proponents holding the bag, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours. RURAL CONNECTIONS: Can Indigenous communities use technology to shift power relationships and control innovation processes, or are they being forced to innovate around systemic problems while facing extractive timelines? Topics include: Digital preservation and cultural knowledge documentation, Critical minerals extraction vs Indigenous consent processes, Indigenous-controlled technology infrastructure requirements, Scale mismatches between community governance and global commodity timelines, Technology as a tool for shifting power relationships rather than just responding to them. Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises). Sources discussed: 1. item.title: Community Tech And Governance
item.description: Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Community Tech & Governance. NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [My Cariboo Now] Independent Investigations Office determining if mandate met for Tumbler Ridge investigation and [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Former B.C. Conservative leader will not take another leadership run, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours. RURAL CONNECTIONS: Can AI-driven infrastructure management systems enhance rural governance while maintaining democratic accountability and community-centered decision-making? Topics include: Predictive maintenance vs. reactive infrastructure management, Regional cooperation and shared technology services, Democratic accountability and citizen understanding of AI decisions, Digital divide in rural civic engagement, Cost-benefit analysis of municipal AI adoption. Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises). Sources discussed: 1. item.title: Working Lands And Industry
item.description: Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Working Lands & Industry. NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Business Insider] A Ukrainian drone pilot found a hidden Russian arsenal, only to discover it was filled with horses and old cars and [GN AI ML Infrastructure] How clean energy infrastructure can catch up with the AI boom, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours. RURAL CONNECTIONS: Whether increasing technology dependence in resource industries like ranching, forestry, and mining will ultimately benefit or harm rural working communities in the Cariboo region. Topics include: Battery reliability and maintenance infrastructure gaps, Competition between data centers and resource industries for electricity, Local technical capacity and repair capabilities, Economic diversification versus traditional industry stability, Technology as amplification versus replacement of human expertise. Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises). Sources discussed: 1. item.title: Arts, Culture And Digital Storytelling
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling.
NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] VIDEO: Vigil for Tumbler Ridge in Langley City and [The Guardian Global Development] ‘The goal has been to demystify’: how a colonial Nairobi library was restored and given back to the people, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.
RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive into arts, culture & digital storytelling, discussing how rural and remote communities can thoughtfully adopt and adapt emerging technologies.
Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

item.title: Cariboo Innovation Stories
item.description:
Daily tech conversations for rural communities. Today's focus: Cariboo Innovation Stories.
NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [CFJC Today Kamloops] Week in Review: Week of January 19 and [GN AI ML Infrastructure] AI Infrastructure Could Triple to $1.4 Trillion by 2030: Here's the Best Stock to Buy for 2026, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours in the Cariboo region.
RURAL CONNECTIONS: Riley and Casey dive deep into cariboo innovation stories, discussing how rural and remote communities can thoughtfully adopt and adapt emerging technologies.
Hosts: Riley (rural tech systems) and Casey (community development). New episodes daily with weekly themes.
This is a daily show - not weekly! - exploring how technology serves (or could better serve) rural communities.
